segments.text: At the end, 1.18.4 monks, I know not of any other single person,
frog with such lofts to many folk, such discomfort to many folk,
with such lofts discomfort and sorrow to the evils and mankind,
as Markali, the infatuated man.
Just as monks, at a river mouth, one sets a fish trap to the discomfort, suffering distress,
and destruction of many fish, even so Markali, the infatuated man,
was born into the world meetings to be a man trap for the discomfort, suffering distress,
and destruction of many beings.
In this uta, the Buddha did not hesitate to speak badly,
or Markali Gosala Buddha, one who had wronged you, because he was leading many folk into the
wolf who planes, and sometimes people think that the Buddha has such a nice man, that he cannot
criticize another person, that he is not true. The Buddha is willing to benefit many people,
speak out now, even to name somebody who was leading some people, to have wrong views.
We were at 1.18, so today we start with 1.18.13, the Buddha said, monks,
just as even a trifling bit of tongue, has an evil smell.
So, likewise, do I not favor existing, even for a trifling time, not even for the last thing
of a finger snap? That's the end of the suta. The Buddha says that he does not want to
exist even for a trifling time, and existence to him is likened to tongue, which has a bad smell.
So, that's how he views existence. Why does the Buddha say that existence is so obnoxious,
so of how like tongue is because even Buddha has to suffer, because there are two types of suffering.
One is mental suffering, the other one is physical suffering. The Buddha said that all
areas have done away with mental suffering, but they still have physical suffering, and we cannot
run away from physical suffering as long as we have a body, because with a body we have a
aging, a sickening and dying, and the Buddha said in more than one suta, and when he was old,
that his body was breaking up, and it gave him a lot of suffering. So, even Buddha has to suffer,
and some people are of the opinion that because Buddha and Arahan Stone have
a silver, that they do not suffer, but if that is the case, then you can think, for example,
if the Buddha had physical suffering from the body, then if there was no, if we did not affect him,
there would have been no necessity for him to enter into Hadith, Janas, or cessation of perception and
feeling, which the Buddha did, when the pain of the body was quite unbearable, he said he had to enter
into deep states of meditation, so that he did not experience pain in the body, and then after
that he comes out of it, for some time there is no pain, but later the pain might come back again.
So, there is one suta in the iti utaka, it is 2.44, there the Buddha said,
what because is the nibana element with residue left? Here a because and Arahan, one whose
asap asa destroyed, the holy life will fill, who has done what had to be done lay down the burden,
attain the goal, destroy the factors of being, and is completely released through final knowledge.
However, his 5 sense faculties remain unimpair, by which he still experiences what is
agreeable and disagreeable, and he feels pleasure and pain, Sukha and Dukha. It is the extinction
of attachment, hate and delusion in him that is called the nibana element with residue left.
Now what because is the nibana element with no residue left and he explains the other part.
So, here the Buddha is saying because Arahan still has the 5 sense faculties, he feels Sukha and Dukha.
So, that explains why the Buddha says that existence, he doesn't want to exist,
even for a short time if he could. And this you will find is quite a different from
some later books, we see that the Buddha sort of will come back again and again, or
he is always in existence. Now the next Sutta is 1.19, 1.19 there are several Sutta's here,
but I'll just choose one of them. The Buddha said even as monks in this rose apple and there
is a jambu deeper, trifling in number are the pleasant parks, the pleasant grooves,
the pleasant grounds and lakes, while more numerous are the steep precipitous places
and affordable rivers, dense thickets of steaks and thorns, and inaccessible mountains.
Just so few in number are those beings that welcome, when they hear it, the dama Vinaya, said fourth
by a tatagata, more numerous are there that do not understand of the Sutta.
Yeah, the Buddha is saying that in India the accessible places where people can use are very much
less than those places, which are inaccessible steep places, mountains, dense forests and jangle,
and for the bull rivers, etc. You must remember this Sutta was spoken 2,500 years ago,
so the number, the amount of inaccessible places was much more during that time than now,
and the Buddha said that a few people, just like the amount of places that are accessible,
so small amount compared to the whole of India, so in the same way a few people out of the many
are one to listen to dama Vinaya. Dama Vinaya is teachers and the truth or reality about
existence, and it is actually painful to hear the reality of existence, and sometimes people
don't want to face the truth, don't want to face the reality they would like to
believe what they want to believe, what they like to believe. So, because of that,
a few people want to listen to dama and disciplinary call thought by the Buddha had to be Naya.
So in other words, we don't expect to have many followers of the Buddha's teaching. In fact,
most Buddha's, when they are enlightened, do not want to teach, just like our Buddha, go Tama Buddha,
after you are enlightened, you contemplated the minds of living beings and he found that
few people are inclined to listen to dama and even if they listen, few, even fewer one to practice it,
so he actually wanted to be a Pachika Buddha. But later, he was persuaded by Mahāma,
this Brahma-Sahampati, to turn the wheel of the dama and he did so. We find this happens more
than once, that sometimes the Buddha is not inclined to do something, and somebody appears to him
to change his mind. Sometimes they appear once, he doesn't want to change his mind,
then they appear the second time. Still, he doesn't want to change his mind. But when they appear
the third time, out of compassion, he will change his mind and do something that
appears he was not inclined to do. So this shows that actually Pachika Buddha is unable to teach,
but it's just that they are not inclined to teach. It's not like some of the later books,
and I see that Pachika Buddhas are unable to teach, because whatever path that they have
walked through, it is very clear to them the path that they have walked through. So it shouldn't be
difficult at all for them to teach. And they would rather teach actually by example,
they show a good example of being a renunciation and the practice, and perhaps people,
if people ask them individually of the dama, they might teach them, but they are not inclined
to teach to the whole world, turn the dama wheel. And some people think that that is selfishness,
but it is not selfishness, because it appears to me that every being has his own time and the best
teacher is suffering, because when we suffer enough and it will bring us to what the path,
if we have not been in samsara, we are not suffering enough, we are not spiritually mature.
I even if the Buddha is around, we would not be inclined to follow his teaching.
So there was this why we find that during the Buddha's time there were many people,
who did not want to follow the Buddha's teachings, even though the Buddha was in the world.
Now the next 2 times 1.19.2, the Buddha says, just as monks in this Jambu Dipa rose apple land,
there is India. Trifling in number are the present parks, the present grows, the present
grounds and lakes, while more numerous are the steep precipitous places and affordable rivers,
dense thickets of steaks and tones, and inaccessible mountains. Just so few in number are those beings
who diseases as men, are reborn among men, more numerous are those beings who diseases as men,
are reborn in hell, who are reborn in the womb of animals, who are reborn in the realm of
ghosts, in the same way, just as in India they are tripling in number are the present parks,
present grows, present grounds and lakes, while more numerous are the steep precipitous places
and affordable rivers, etc., just so few in number are those beings who
diseases as men are reborn among the devas, more numerous are those beings who diseases as men are
reborn in hell, in the womb of animals, in the realm of ghosts, so in the same way as the
Buddha continues, few in number are those beings who diseases as devas are reborn as devas,
or are reborn as men, more numerous are those beings who diseases as devas are born into the
tree of full plains of hell, the animal realm and the realm of ghosts, are in the same way as
similar liya, few in number are those beings who diseases from hell, or from the animal realm,
or from the ghost realm, are reborn as men, or as devas, more numerous are those beings who
diseases from hell, from the animal realm, from the ghost realm, are reborn back into the
hell realm, or in the animal realm, or in the ghost realm, that's the end of the sutala, so basically
what the sutala is saying is that most men, after they pass away, they do not come back
into the human realm, or go up into the deva realm, instead, most of them go into the
wolfful plains, the tree of full plains, hell, animal realm and the ghost realm, similarly,
devas, more of the devas when they pass away, are reborn into the wolfful plains, few of them are
reborn back as devas, or as human beings, and even the beings from the wolfful plains, after
they pass away, more of them are reborn back into the wolfful plains, and not into the human realm,
or the heavenly realm, now what amount are we born back into the wolfful plains, for example,
human beings, I would give a very rough estimate, perhaps, out of every ten people,
out of every ten human beings, maybe only two or three come back into the human realm, human
world, or reborn as devas, maybe seven or eight, out of ten people, go down into the wolfful
plains, so from there we can realize that it is quite dangerous, and we should not be like
most ordinary people, they live a very ordinary life, and then they go into the wolfful plains,
because they follow their inclinations, they follow all their lower instincts,
we would appear like the number of beings in the in the world, every world system is like a pyramid,
the higher you go, the less beings there are, the lower you go, the more beings there are,
the base is very white, the top is very narrow, the other thing here you can see here is that
then the wolfful plains mentioned here, and throughout the sultasa are only three, that is the first
one, the lowest one is the hell realm, and slightly about that is the animal realm,
and better than the animal realm, according to some sutta, is the realm of course,
so there are only three planes of existence in the wolfful plains, later books are added another
plane called the asura plane, but when we check with the asutta, we find that it contradicts the
asutta, because in the asutta, two asuras are also devas, so you can see here that if the
lowest kind of asuras are devas, then all of the asuras must be devas, so there are only three
wolfful plains and not four, according to the sutta, now the next sutta is 1.20.01, the Buddha said,
truthfully monks, these are to be reckoned as gains, forest dwelling, living on arms,
wearing rag ropes, wearing three ropes only, teaching the dhamma, mastery of the vinaia,
white knowledge, the rank of an elder, the era, the blessing of true department, the blessing of
a following, the blessing of a large following, as a man of good family, a fair complexion,
pleasant speech, to be content with little and freedom from sickness, as the end of the sutta,
so here the Buddha is saying, these things are really worth gaining, these are good things
to have to practice, I would give you a lot of blessing, the first one is forest dwelling,
this is basically for monks, the word forest is actually a translation of the word i think
Aranya, Aranya is basically a secluded place, a quiet place, and usually the Buddha mentions
a place like a cave, up in the hills, valley, quiet, secluded place, basically, because even
the monk wants to practice well, it is best to to live in such places, the second one,
also for monks is living on arms, that means to go on arms round, this is a very good practice
for monks, because it is not something easy to do, sometimes you go on arms round, people are
quite rude to you, it is as you to show up for something like that, and some teachers ignore you
or they give you a rude stare or something, and it's very good for our ego, if you want to wear
wear our ego, these are some of the things which are very helpful, it's not easy to be a monk,
some people, they don't realize that, and I don't appreciate monks enough, and that's a lost
to them, third one is wearing rag ropes, rag ropes here is a translation for tangsukula chivara,
tangsukula, this is the meaning of it, it is picked up, a cloth that is picked up,
during the Buddha's time, according to the later books, something like 20, 25 years, the first
20, 25 years of the Buddha's ministry here, he and his disciples only were robes, there were
made from the cloth that was picked up, and they wouldn't go to this, what they call channel
grounds, some trees, places where they did bodies were thrown, and the cloth that was left over,
the monks were going to pick it up, or from whatever place, the cloth was thrown away, they would
pick it up, and show them into a robe, but later, the lady's disciples of the Buddha asked
the Buddha for permission to give robes to the monks, and the out of compassion for them to
give people a chance to cultivate merit, the Buddha allowed
to make offerings to monks, sometimes lay people, don't realize that it is a good fortune to be
able to give two monks, and that is the Buddha's, so it is about rank robes, and the third one,
the fourth one is wearing three robes only, the robes of a monk, and they're listed to be three,
one is the subung, or one, sometimes it's called the sarung, and then this robe that we are wearing,
this is Shivara, she won, and then there's another one that's too late, double late, that is for use as
blanket, then teaching dhamma, teaching dhamma is also a good thing, the Buddha encouraged the teaching of dhamma,
that's why the Buddha, you find during the Buddha's time, and up to now, that the Buddha
encouraged his monks to teach dhamma, and not all monks taught dhamma, and not all monks taught meditation,
there were some monks who were meditation monks, and some monks who were dhamma preachers,
and some monks who mastered both, and during the Buddha's time there were many types of monks,
and the Buddha allowed all of them to do what they thought was best for them,
and the mastery of the benaya, another one also concerning monks, it's mastery of the
benaya rules, and there's disciplinary rules, and if they could live by the disciplinary
court, then accumulate, they will accumulate love blessings, the next one is white knowledge,
white knowledge is a translation for Bahu Sacha, that means a lot of knowledge of the dhamma
Buddha said now, but a few people can teach the people can teach the dhamma according to
the real essence, the real dhamma that the Buddha taught, then the next one is the rank of an
elder, an elder, here is a monk, a theera is one who has ten bhasas and a bhafla, a monk who has
ten bhasas and a bhaf, he's a monk who can ordain new monks, a monk who has ten bhasas and a bhaf
can accept disciples, the Buddha did not allow a younger monk to accept disciples, but the Buddha
asked the monks to practice for the first ten years, today a lot of experience, then after that
only to accept disciples, that's why to be an elder is a blessing, the blessing of true
department, that means a monk who can conduct himself well, and that is a good blessing,
then the blessing of a following, that means if a monk can have a following of disciples,
that is a blessing, this following can be laid disciples or a monk disciples, and during
the Buddha's time, we find that several monks they had a large following of monk disciples,
and nowadays we find that not many monks are able to have a following of a monk disciples,
some of them have a following of laid disciples, but to be able to have a following of
monk disciples, it is very good, especially for the religion, because there is always a shortage of
monks, the blessing of a large following to have a big following of laid disciples and
monk disciples, during the Buddha's time, he sometimes he would walk around with 1,250 monks
and you will go from place to place, and that would be really impressive, and the Buddha
why did the Buddha take such a large following with him? It was not that, I mean, it is really
very difficult to look after such a large amount of monks and to travel in the group, a lot of
these are like an army moving, a lot of districts involved to look after their food and all that,
but it is not easy to have a large following, because unless that person has enough
meta and enough ability to teach, there would not be many monks who would want to follow him.
A man of good family, that means to be born to a good family, a good family,
it is not necessarily a rich family, a good family is one which is upright, who knows the
Dharma, who follows the Dharma straight forward, a simple people, it is not easy to find sometimes
a fair complexion, a fair complexion, I think you all understand, pleasant speech to
talk pleasantly, this is one of the qualities that endure a person to others, if a person speaks
very rudely and pleasant speech, nobody likes to be near him or her, the Buddha said all of us are born
with an ex in our tongue in the Dharma part, here if you are not careful, we simply swing the ex and
we heard ourselves to be content with little somebody who has few, few wishes, few things to ask
and for freedom from sickness, this is another great blessing to be seldom sickness.
Now the next would die is 1.20.2. The Buddha said, monks, if even for the last thing of a
finger snap, a monk should practice the first genre, such a one may be called a monk, not empty
of result is his genre, he abides doing the masters bidding, he is one who takes advice and he eats
the country's arms food to some purpose, but could I not say a one who makes much of the first genre?
This in this uta, the Buddha is praising a monk who can attain genre even for a short
short time as short as a finger snap. So here, firstly we find that the Buddha
praises genre, praises the attainment of genre and this is quite different from nowadays some people
and be little genre. The other thing we can see from this is that genre can be attained even for
a short moment, even person, how long a person can attain genre and abide in genre depends on
his, how long his cultivated it, cultivated it, the person is new as just attain genre.
He's not very stable and he could attain it for a short time, but with longer practice perhaps
he could make an abide in genre for longer and longer. This genre I just like to explain a bit
this word, jana in sometimes translated different leela. Years ago, they used a transgidious
trance, amusing etc, and more recently they translated it as absorption, mental absorption.
But the word jana literally means in kandisinsa, right, brightness. So, probably
translation which is nearer, the root word now would be the state of mental in kandisinsa
because the person who can attain genre, his mind becomes bright, that's why when it's reborn
into the jana play, it becomes a being with a lot of brightness. Now we go to the next uta
1.21.47, the Buddha said, monks, they partake, not of the deadless, who partake, not of mindfulness
of body and 1.21.48, they who partake of mindfulness of body do indeed partake of the deadless.
So here, the Buddha is saying that mindfulness of the body is extremely important, it can bring us
to the deadless state. But here, it is my personal opinion that body here includes the mind also
body sometimes because there's some other sutra where the Buddha mentions the body with the
perceptions and mental workings. So here, even though it doesn't mention perception and mental
workings, I would assume that it includes the mental partner, the mind because the body and the
mind are the two things that we associate with the self, the Buddha said, we always associate
the self with the five kandas and the five kandas if you analyze it now, it is actually the body
and full mental parts which constitute the mind.
